controls.primaryFailureMode: Scope escape before or during writes.

quick-fix.backstop: Human diff review is weaker because it happens after the write.

lightweight.backstop: Human diff review is weaker because it happens after the write.

guarded.backstop: No cheaper backstop catches guarded scope escape before the write.

full-bmad.backstop: No cheaper backstop catches BMAD scope escape before the write.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Chat-memory drift, stale story, wrong phase, or wrong active ledger.

quick-fix.backstop: A tight single-context quick fix can use explicit user scope plus focused repro evidence.

lightweight.backstop: Manual conductor ledger checks are slower and easier to skip.

guarded.backstop: Manual checks are not enough for guarded multi-actor dispatch.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual checks are not enough for full-BMAD multi-phase dispatch.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Wrong next action, phase jump, or repeated full-ledger reread.

quick-fix.backstop: Explicit quick-fix envelope and focused evidence can be enough for a tight single-context fix.

lightweight.backstop: Manual next-step checks are slower and less reproducible.

guarded.backstop: Manual next-step checks are not enough for guarded work.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual next-step checks are not enough for full-BMAD work.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Self-review, role collapse, or unverifiable separation.

quick-fix.backstop: Real separate-worker topology plus reviewer report, with weaker attribution.

lightweight.backstop: Real separate-worker topology plus reviewer report, with weaker attribution.

guarded.backstop: Reviewer/conductor reports alone do not make separation auditable.

full-bmad.backstop: Reviewer/conductor reports alone do not make separation auditable.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Ambiguous continuation text treated as new scope approval.

quick-fix.backstop: Fresh explicit user confirmation before widening the quick-fix envelope.

lightweight.backstop: Fresh explicit user confirmation before scope expansion.

guarded.backstop: Fresh explicit user confirmation before guarded start or expansion.

full-bmad.backstop: Fresh explicit user confirmation before epic transition or expansion.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Stale output, wrong command, wrong commit, or chat-only evidence.

quick-fix.backstop: Skeptical disk re-run of focused repro and verification commands.

lightweight.backstop: Skeptical disk re-run plus lightweight surface evidence.

guarded.backstop: Manual re-run can audit but should not replace guarded evidence manifests.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual re-run can audit but should not replace full-BMAD evidence manifests.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Evidence record edited after command or detached from raw output.

quick-fix.backstop: Skeptical re-run and raw log inspection.

lightweight.backstop: Skeptical re-run and raw log inspection.

guarded.backstop: Raw log inspection can audit but should not replace guarded runner proof.

full-bmad.backstop: Raw log inspection can audit but should not replace full-BMAD runner proof.

controls.primaryFailureMode: PASS without required evidence or with the wrong decision actor.

quick-fix.backstop: Manual closeout checklist plus independent review.

lightweight.backstop: Manual closeout checklist plus independent review.

guarded.backstop: Manual closeout is weaker for guarded PASS decisions.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual closeout is weaker for full-BMAD PASS decisions.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Fake runtime proof, in-memory persistence, or unreachable UI.

quick-fix.backstop: Manual browser/runtime check with durable persistence proof.

lightweight.backstop: Manual browser/runtime check with durable persistence proof.

guarded.backstop: Manual browser/runtime check with durable persistence proof.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual browser/runtime check with durable persistence proof.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Green-by-weakening-tests when a worker edits tests.

quick-fix.backstop: Reviewer diff of assertions, skips, matchers, and implementation references.

lightweight.backstop: Reviewer diff of assertions, skips, matchers, and implementation references.

guarded.backstop: Reviewer diff is useful but should not replace guarded test-integrity evidence.

full-bmad.backstop: Reviewer diff is useful but should not replace full-BMAD test-integrity evidence.

controls.primaryFailureMode: Closeout evidence lives in chat or asks small lanes for full multi-actor paperwork.

quick-fix.backstop: Focused quick-fix repro, before-patch, after-patch, and session handoff evidence.

lightweight.backstop: Lightweight lifecycle artifact, focused verification evidence, and session handoff.

guarded.backstop: Manual audit can supplement but should not replace guarded closeout evidence.

full-bmad.backstop: Manual audit can supplement but should not replace full-BMAD closeout evidence.

notes: This manifest defines control dose by lane. It does not replace the lane validators that verify actual run artifacts.
